rules.details: Corsair (Type 4) and Seasonic use completely different modular pinouts on the power supply side. Plugging a Seasonic cable into a Corsair PSU (or vice-versa) will short circuit your motherboard, GPU, or CPU.
EVGA and Corsair modular ports are pin-incompatible. Mixing cables will likely trigger over-current/short-circuit protection or cause permanent hardware damage.
Many ASUS ROG/Strix power supplies use Seasonic as their OEM and share the Seasonic pinout, but some use Corsair custom pinouts. Double check the specific PSU generation before mixing.
Seasonic and EVGA modular cables have incompatible layouts on the PSU side. Mixing will result in electrical shorts.
Most ASUS modular PSUs (Strix, Thor) are built on Seasonic platforms and share Seasonic pinout standards. Confirm your model's OEM to ensure safety.
